---
title: "Chapter 1 - Arjuna Vishada Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">अर्जुनविषादयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><span></span><a href="chapter-02.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-1-1" data-reference="1.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-1">1.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Dhritarashtra)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">On the field of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, at Kurukshetra, gathered and eager for battle—what did my men and the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pandavas</span> do, O Sanjaya?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः ।<br>
मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-2" data-reference="1.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-2">1.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Then, seeing the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pandava</span> army drawn up in formation, King Duryodhana approached his teacher and spoke these words.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा ।<br>
आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-3" data-reference="1.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-3">1.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Behold, O teacher, this great army of the sons of Pandu, arrayed by your skilled disciple, the son of Drupada.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम् ।<br>
व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-4" data-reference="1.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-4">1.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Here are heroes, mighty archers, equal in battle to Bhima and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>: Yuyudhana, Virata, and Drupada, the great chariot-warrior.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि ।<br>
युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-5" data-reference="1.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-5">1.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Dhrishtaketu, Chekitana, and the valiant king of Kashi; Purujit, Kuntibhoja, and Shaibya, the foremost of men.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितानः काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान् ।<br>
पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गवः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-6" data-reference="1.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-6">1.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The valiant Yudhamanyu and the courageous Uttamauja,<br>
Saubhadra and the sons of Draupadi—all great chariot-warriors.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान् ।<br>
सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-7" data-reference="1.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-7">1.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Know too, O best of the twice-born, those who are foremost among us, the leaders of my army; these I name to you.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम ।<br>
नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-8" data-reference="1.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-8">1.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Yourself, and Bhishma, Karna, and Kripa, ever victorious in battle; Ashvatthama, Vikarna, and Saumadatti as well.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः ।<br>
अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-9" data-reference="1.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-9">1.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And many other heroes, who have staked their lives for my sake, bearing varied weapons—all skilled in war.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अन्ये च बहवः शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविताः ।<br>
नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-10" data-reference="1.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-10">1.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Our force, guarded by Bhishma, is insufficient;<br>
but their force, guarded by Bhima, is sufficient.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम् ।<br>
पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-11" data-reference="1.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-11">1.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Therefore, stationed in your respective positions throughout the divisions, let all of you guard Bhishma above all.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः ।<br>
भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-12" data-reference="1.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-12">1.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">To cheer him, the aged Kuru, the mighty grandsire, roared like a lion and loudly blew his conch.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तस्य सञ्जनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्धः पितामहः ।<br>
सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-13" data-reference="1.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-13">1.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Then conches, drums, kettledrums, and horns suddenly sounded, and the noise was tumultuous.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः ।<br>
सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-14" data-reference="1.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-14">1.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Then, stationed in their great chariot yoked with white horses, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Madhava: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Madhava: Epithet of Krishna." title="Madhava: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="madhava">Madhava</span> and the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pandava</span> blew their divine conches.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ ।<br>
माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-15" data-reference="1.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-15">1.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." aria-label="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." title="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." data-glossary-id="hrishikesha">Hrishikesha</span> blew the Panchajanya, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span> the Devadatta, and Vrikodara, of mighty deeds, blew the great conch Paundra.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनञ्जयः ।<br>
पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-16" data-reference="1.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-16">1.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">King Yudhishthira, son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span>, blew the Anantavijaya; Nakula and Sahadeva, the Sughosha and the Manipushpaka.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।<br>
नकुलः सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-17" data-reference="1.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-17">1.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The king of Kashi, supreme archer, and Shikhandin, the great chariot-warrior; Dhrishtadyumna, Virata, and the unconquered Satyaki.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">काश्यश्च परमेष्वासः शिखण्डी च महारथः ।<br>
धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-18" data-reference="1.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-18">1.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Drupada and the sons of Draupadi, and the strong-armed son of Subhadra—each on every side blew his conch, O lord of the earth.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः पृथिवीपते ।<br>
सौभद्रश्च महाबाहुः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-19" data-reference="1.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-19">1.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">That tumultuous din, resounding through sky and earth, pierced the hearts of Dhritarashtra&#x27;s sons.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत् ।<br>
नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलोऽभ्यनुनादयन् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-20" data-reference="1.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-20">1.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Then, seeing Dhritarashtra&#x27;s sons arrayed, as the clash of weapons was about to begin, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." aria-label="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." title="Pandava: One of the sons or descendants of Pandu; often refers to Arjuna and his brothers." data-glossary-id="pandava">Pandava</span> whose banner bore the monkey took up his bow</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथ व्यवस्थितान्दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान् कपिध्वजः ।<br>
प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसम्पाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डवः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-21" data-reference="1.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-21">1.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">and spoke these words to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." aria-label="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." title="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." data-glossary-id="hrishikesha">Hrishikesha</span>, O king:<br>
&quot;O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Acyuta: Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one." aria-label="Acyuta: Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one." title="Acyuta: Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one." data-glossary-id="acyuta">Acyuta</span>, place my chariot between the two armies,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते ।<br>
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-22" data-reference="1.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-22">1.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">that I may look upon these men standing eager to fight, with whom I must contend in this work of battle.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान् ।<br>
कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन् रणसमुद्यमे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-23" data-reference="1.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-23">1.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I see them gathered here, ready to fight, seeking to please in battle the evil-minded son of Dhritarashtra.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः ।<br>
धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-24" data-reference="1.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-24">1.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus addressed by <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." aria-label="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." title="Gudakesha: Epithet of Arjuna, commonly linked with mastery over sleep." data-glossary-id="gudakesha">Gudakesha</span>, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." aria-label="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." title="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." data-glossary-id="hrishikesha">Hrishikesha</span> placed that finest of chariots between the two armies,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत ।<br>
सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-25" data-reference="1.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-25">1.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">before Bhishma and Drona and all the rulers of the earth, and said: &quot;O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, behold these Kurus gathered here.&quot;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखतः सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम् ।<br>
उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-26" data-reference="1.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-26">1.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">There <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span> saw, standing before him, fathers and grandfathers, teachers, uncles, brothers, sons, grandsons, and friends as well;</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान्पार्थः पितॄनथ पितामहान् ।<br>
आचार्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातॄन्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-27" data-reference="1.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-27">1.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">fathers-in-law and companions too, in both armies. Seeing all these kinsmen arrayed,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि ।<br>
तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-28" data-reference="1.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-28">1.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">the son of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." aria-label="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." title="Kaunteya: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Kunti." data-glossary-id="kaunteya">Kunti</span> was overcome with deep compassion, and in sorrow he said:<br>
<br>
&quot;Seeing my own people, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>, gathered here eager to fight,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कृपया परयाविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत् ।<br>
दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-29" data-reference="1.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-29">1.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">my limbs give way and my mouth goes dry;<br>
my body trembles and my hair stands on end.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति ।<br>
वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-30" data-reference="1.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-30">1.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The Gandiva slips from my hand, and my skin burns all over;<br>
I cannot stand steady, and my mind seems to reel.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते ।<br>
न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-31" data-reference="1.31">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-31">1.31</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And I see adverse omens, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." title="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="keshava">Keshava</span>; I foresee no good in killing my own people in battle.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव ।<br>
न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-32" data-reference="1.32">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-32">1.32</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I long for no victory, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>, nor kingdom, nor pleasures.<br>
What use is a kingdom to us, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Govinda: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Govinda: Epithet of Krishna." title="Govinda: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="govinda">Govinda</span>, or enjoyments, or even life?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च ।<br>
किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-33" data-reference="1.33">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-33">1.33</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those for whose sake we would want kingdom, enjoyments, and pleasures stand here in battle, having renounced their lives and wealth:</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च ।<br>
त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-34" data-reference="1.34">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-34">1.34</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">teachers, fathers, sons, and grandfathers;<br>
uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, and other kinsmen.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः ।<br>
मातुलाः श्वशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः सम्बन्धिनस्तथा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-35" data-reference="1.35">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-35">1.35</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">These I would not wish to kill, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." aria-label="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." title="Madhusudana: Epithet of Krishna: slayer of Madhu." data-glossary-id="madhusudana">Madhusudana</span>, even if killed myself—not even for the sovereignty of the three worlds, much less for the earth.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन ।<br>
अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-36" data-reference="1.36">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-36">1.36</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">What joy would be ours, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." title="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="janardana">Janardana</span>, in killing Dhritarashtra&#x27;s sons? Only sin would fall upon us were we to kill these aggressors.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन ।<br>
पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-37" data-reference="1.37">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-37">1.37</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">So we ought not to kill Dhritarashtra&#x27;s sons, our own kinsmen;<br>
for how could we be happy, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Madhava: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Madhava: Epithet of Krishna." title="Madhava: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="madhava">Madhava</span>, after killing our own people?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान् ।<br>
स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-38" data-reference="1.38">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-38">1.38</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Even if these men, their minds overcome by greed, see no fault in the ruin of a family and no crime in the betrayal of friends,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः ।<br>
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-39" data-reference="1.39">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-39">1.39</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">how should we not know to turn back from this sin, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." title="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="janardana">Janardana</span>, we who clearly see the fault in the ruin of a family?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम् ।<br>
कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-40" data-reference="1.40">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-40">1.40</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When a family is ruined, its ancient dharmas perish;<br>
and when <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span> perishes, adharma overcomes the whole family.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः ।<br>
धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-41" data-reference="1.41">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-41">1.41</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">When adharma prevails, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>, the women of the family are corrupted; and when the women are corrupted, O Varshneya, there arises confusion of order.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः ।<br>
स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-42" data-reference="1.42">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-42">1.42</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This confusion brings only hell for the family and for those who ruin it; their ancestors fall, deprived of the offerings of rice and water.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च ।<br>
पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-43" data-reference="1.43">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-43">1.43</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">By these faults of the family-destroyers, which bring about confusion of varna, the eternal dharmas of community and family are undone.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः ।<br>
उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-44" data-reference="1.44">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-44">1.44</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And we have heard, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." title="Janardana: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="janardana">Janardana</span>, that for those whose family dharmas are destroyed, a dwelling in hell is assured.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन ।<br>
नरके नियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-45" data-reference="1.45">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-45">1.45</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Alas, we are bent on committing a great sin: out of greed for the pleasures of a kingdom, we are prepared to kill our own people.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् ।<br>
यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-46" data-reference="1.46">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-46">1.46</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">It would be better for me if Dhritarashtra&#x27;s sons, weapons in hand, were to kill me unresisting and unarmed in the battle.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः ।<br>
धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-1-47" data-reference="1.47">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-1-47">1.47</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having spoken thus on the field of battle, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span> sank down upon the seat of the chariot,<br>
casting away his bow and arrows, his mind stricken with grief.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एवमुक्त्वार्जुनः सङ्ख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् ।<br>
विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
